channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sourcegraph unveils AI coding agents
item.description: Looking to challenge well-established GitHub Copilot in AI-powered coding assistance, Sourcegraph has announced coding agents for code review, code migration, testing, documentation, and notification, along with an API for building custom agents. Sourcegraph made the announcement on January 29.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft first raises doubts about DeepSeek and then adds it to its cloud
item.description: Despite initiating a probe into Chinese AI startup DeepSeek, Microsoft has added the startup’s latest reasoning model R1, to its model catalog on Azure AI Foundry and GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Record levels of 'forever chemicals' found in wildlife near New Mexico base, hunters warned
item.description: Environmental regulators and health officials in New Mexico are warning hunters that harmful chemicals known to cause cancer in people have been found at record levels in birds, small mammals and plants at a lake near Holloman Air Force Base.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta agrees to pay $25 million to settle lawsuit from Trump after Jan. 6 suspension
item.description: Meta has agreed to pay $25 million to settle a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump against the company after it suspended his accounts following the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lutnick says he won’t dismantle NOAA as called for in Project 2025
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead the Commerce Department assured senators on Wednesday he won’t try to dismantle the nation’s climate, oceans and weather science agency during a largely genial confirmation hearing. Sen. Maria Cantwell (D-Wash.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: 3 key areas where AI is transforming insurance today
item.description: In the two years since generative artificial intelligence (genAI) burst onto the global stage and quickly found its way into the strategy playbooks of the world’s largest businesses, we’ve all heard a lot about the technology’s potential.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The end of fake VPNs? Google Play Store now shows which VPNs are secure enough to be trusted
item.description: Google's new "Verified" is designed to highlight VPN apps that prioritize user privacy and safety. Here's how it works.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: DeepSeek underscores US-China race for AI dominance, OpenAI says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI poised to overhaul the customer journey
item.description: AI is poised to take over the customer journey for a wide range of products in the next five years. CIOs who ignore the trend risk losing major ground to competitors, AI and marketing experts suggest.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: World’s largest pulp manufacturer streamlined SAP S/4HANA integration for better outcomes
item.description: Cloud-based enterprise resource planning solutions are transforming and enhancing business operations. Yet despite the organisational benefits, businesses are slow to modernise IT, citing challenges in integrating legacy systems due to change management and user adoption issues.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 breaks the sound barrier—the first private aircraft to do so
item.description: About 35,000 feet (10,670 meters) over the Mojave Desert, northwest of Los Angeles, Boom Supersonic’s XB-1 became the first privately funded airplane to break the sound barrier during a test flight on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers
item.description: Steve Nulter, a senior business analyst in the Quality Service Management Office, said more agencies are using financial management shared services. The post Financial management QSMO growing in use, providers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Toll Road Scam Targets US Residents: How to Protect Yourself
item.description: A new scam targeting US residents involves text messages claiming unpaid tolls and fines, with scammers asking for card details and one-time passwords to steal money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Endor Labs’ new tool helps enterprises track the AI models they use
item.description: Software supply chain security vendor Endor Labs has added another dimension to its products with Tuesday’s launch of AI Model Discovery, which will be part of its core open-source evaluation offering.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Everything wrong with the AI landscape in 2025, hilariously captured in this ‘SNL’ sketch
item.description: “Isn’t AI supposed to make things simpler?” asks a student in a new Saturday Night Live sketch. Technically, the answer is yes. Artificial intelligence is often pitched as a future-forward omni-tool for removing friction from everyday tasks.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Update Exchange Server or move to the cloud, say experts
item.description: Microsoft Exchange administrators running versions older than March 2023 need to update or they won’t get the latest security mitigations, says an expert. But, David Shipley added, even better advice is to shift quickly to the cloud-based Microsoft 365, which always has the latest patches.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Puppet open source fork OpenVox arrives
item.description: The Vox Pupuli collective group has announced the first release of OpenVox, a community-maintained open source implementation of the Puppet configuration management system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, Fox 5
